sections.title: Starting the quest
Talking to Edmond

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello old man.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Sorry, I can't stop to talk...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I've got to find my daughter. I pray that she is still alive...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's happened to her?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Elena's a healer. Three weeks ago she managed to cross the wall into West Ardougne. No one's allowed to cross the wall in case they spread the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Plague?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Not that long ago, West Ardougne was hit by a deadly plague. They had the wall built to try and keep it contained. No one is allowed to enter the city now apart from the mourners.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: They say the plague is a horrible way to go... That's why Elena felt she had to go help. She said she'd be gone for a few days but we've heard nothing since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help find her?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Really, would you? I've been working on a plan to get into West Ardougne, but I'm too old and tired to carry it through. But you on the other hand, you should have no problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where should I start?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: If you're going into West Ardougne you'll need protection from the plague. My wife made a special gas mask for Elena with dwellberries rubbed into it.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: They help to repel the plague apparently. We need some more though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find these dwellberries?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: The only place I know of is McGrubor's Wood, just west of Seers' Village. The berries are bright blue so they're easy to spot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and get some.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: The foresters keep a close eye on it, but there is a back way in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts, I'd better not.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Well if you hear anything about Elena please tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck finding her.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond before getting dwellberries

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Edmond.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Have you got the dwellberries yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: You'll probably find them in McGrubor's Wood, just west of Seers' Village. The berries are bright blue so they're easy to spot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and get some.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: The foresters keep a close eye on it, but there is a back way in.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena before getting dwellberries

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Edmond has asked me to help find your daughter.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Yes he told me. I've begun making your special gas mask, but I need some dwellberries to finish it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try to get some.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: The best place to look is in McGrubor's Wood, just west of Seers' Village.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after getting dwellberries

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Edmond.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Have you got the dwellberries yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I've got some here.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Take them to my wife Alrena, she's inside.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after getting dwellberries

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Edmond has asked me to help find your daughter.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Yes he told me. I've begun making your special gas mask, but I need some dwellberries to finish it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I've got some here.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: There we go, all done. While in West Ardougne you must wear this at all times, or you could catch the plague.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: I'll make a spare mask for you in case you lose that one. I'll hide it in the cupboard in case the mourners come looking.

sections.title: Searching the cupboard
Talking to Alrena after getting the gas mask

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello darling, I think Edmond had a good idea of how to get into West Ardougne, you should hear his idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go and see him now.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after getting the gas mask

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Edmond, I've got the gas mask now.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Good stuff, now for the digging. Beneath us are the Ardougne sewers. I've done some research, and I reckon you can use them to enter West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I've already tried digging down to them but the soil is rock hard. You'll need to pour on several buckets of water to soften it up. I reckon four buckets should do it.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond before pouring water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to pour four more buckets of water on the soil.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena before pouring water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello darling, how's that tunnel coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need to soften the soil and then we'll start digging.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Well I'm sure you're quicker than Edmond.

sections.title: Digging the mud patch
Interacting with the mud patch
Using bucket of water on the mud patch
Digging hole to make a tunnel
Climbing up the mud pile
Climbing down dug hole
Talking to Mourner outside Edmond's house

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You and that Edmond fella, you're looking very suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're just gardening. Have you heard any news about West Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Just the usual, everyone's sick or dying. I'm furious at King Tyras for bringing this plague to our lands.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after pouring one bucket of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to pour three more buckets of water on the soil.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after pouring one bucket of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello darling, how's that tunnel coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need to soften the soil a little more and then we'll start digging.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after pouring two buckets of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to pour two more buckets of water on the soil.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after pouring three buckets of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to pour one more bucket of water on the soil.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after pouring four buckets of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've soaked the soil with water.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: That's great, it should be soft enough to dig through now. There should be a spade nearby that you can use.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after pouring four buckets of water on the mud patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: How's the tunnel going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting there.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: One of the mourners has been sniffing around asking questions about you and Edmond, you should keep an eye out for him.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Also, don't forget about that spare gas mask if you need it. It's hidden in the cupboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks Alrena!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the warning.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner outside Edmond's house after talking with Edmond

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What are you up to with old man Edmond?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, we've just been chatting.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What about his daughter?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you know about that then?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: We know about everything that goes on in Ardougne. We have to if we are to contain the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you see his daughter recently?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I imagine she's caught the plague. Either way she won't be allowed out of West Ardougne, the risk is too great.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond in the tunnel

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I think it's the pipe to the south that comes up in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright I'll check it out.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after digging the tunnel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hi, have you managed to get through to West Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I should be going soon.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Make sure you wear your mask while you're over there! I can't think of a worse way to die.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Don't forget, I've got a spare one hidden in the cupboard if you need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks Alrena!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the warning.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner outside Edmond's house after digging the tunnel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Been digging have we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Your hands are covered in mud.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh that...

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Funny, you don't look like the gardening type.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, I love gardening! It's my favorite pastime.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner outside Edmond's house after pulling off the grate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I don't trust you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't have to.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: If I find you attempting to cross the wall I'll make sure you never return.

sections.title: Interacting with the pipe
Climb-up pipe

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I can't let you enter the city without your gas mask on.

sections.title: Open grill
Using rope on grill
Talking to Edmond after trying to pull the grill

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Edmond, I can't get through to West Ardougne! There's an iron grill blocking my way, I can't pull it off alone.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: If you get some rope you could tie to the grill, then we could both pull it at the same time.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after tying the rope to the grill on the pipe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've tied a rope to the grill over there, will you help me pull it off?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Alright, let's get to it...

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Once you're in the city look for a man called Jethick. He's an old friend of the family. Hopefully he can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, thanks I will.

sections.title: Entering West Ardougne
Talking to Edmond after unlocking West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Have you found Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, it's a big city over there.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: I hope it's not too late.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after unlocking West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello, any word on Elena?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Is there anything else I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's alright, I'll get her back soon.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: That's the spirit, dear. Don't forget that there's a spare gas mask in the cupboard if you need one.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: That's the spirit, dear.

sections.title: Interacting with the manhole in West Ardougne
Climb-down manhole out of West Ardougne
Opening manhole cover
Closing manhole cover
Opening doors to leave West Ardougne
Talking to Men and Women in West Ardougne
Woman (variant 1)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Bah, those mourners... they're meant to be helping us, but I think they're doing more harm here than good. They won't even let me send a letter out to my family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a lady called Elena around here?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Yes, I've seen her. Very helpful person.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Not for the last few days though... I thought maybe she'd gone home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should stand up to them more.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh I'm not one to cause a fuss.

sections.title: Woman (variant 2)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Life is tough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, living in a plague city must be hard.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Plague? Pah, that's no excuse for the treatment we've received. It's obvious pretty quickly if someone has the plague.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'm thinking about making a break for it. I'm perfectly healthy, not gonna infect anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Well, ain't much either you or me can do about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a lady called Elena.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I've not heard of her. Old Jethick knows a lot of people, maybe he'll know where you can find her.

sections.title: Woman (variant 3)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: We don't have good days here anymore. Curse King Tyras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh okay, bad day then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what has he done?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: His army curses our city with this plague then wanders off again, leaving us to clear up the pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman called Elena.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Not heard of her.

sections.title: Woman (variant 4)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: An outsider! Can you get me out of this hell hole?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, that's not what I'm here to do.

sections.title: Man (variant 1)

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: We don't have good days here anymore. Curse King Tyras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh okay, bad day then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what has he done?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: His army curses our city with this plague then wanders off again, leaving us to clear up the pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman called Elena.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Not heard of her.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner or Head Mourner in West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Hmmm, how did you get over here? You're not one of this rabble. Ah well, you'll have to stay. Can't risk you going back now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's a mourner?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: We're working for King Lathas of East Ardougne. He has tasked us with containing the accursed plague sweeping West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: We also do our best to ease these people's suffering. We're nicknamed mourners because we spend a lot of time at plague victim funerals, no one else is allowed to risk attending.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: It's a demanding job, and we get little thanks from the people here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got the plague though...

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Can't risk you being a carrier. That protective clothing you have isn't regulation issue. It won't meet safety standards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman named Elena.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Ah yes, I've heard of her. A healer I believe. She must be mad coming over here voluntarily.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I hear rumours she has probably caught the plague now. Very tragic, a stupid waste of life.

sections.title: Talking to Ted Rehnison before getting the book from Jethick

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Go away. We don't want any.

sections.title: Talking to Jethick if the player does not have Elena's picture

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hello, I don't recognise you. We don't get many newcomers around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: The plague of course. Not many people want to come to a place like this and the few that do normally get stopped by the mourners.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: All you'll find here now are the dead and the dying. Even our own king has abandoned us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your king?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Yes, King Tyras of West Ardougne. He's the brother of King Lathas, the ruler of East Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is the king?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Well he's always been a bit of an explorer. He's led multiple expeditions into the uncharted lands to the west.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: The plague first started when he came back from one of these expeditions. More than a few suspect that some of his men caught it out there and brought it back with them.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: The king didn't care though. He just left on another expedition to the west. He hasn't been seen since. He left the city warder Bravek in charge but he's no better.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Anyway, you clearly didn't come here to talk about kings. So tell me, what brings you to West Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman from East Ardougne called Elena.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: East Ardougnian women are easier to find in East Ardougne. Not many would come to West Ardougne to find one. Although the name is familiar, what does she look like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... brown hair... in her twenties...

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hmmm, that doesn't narrow it down a huge amount... I'll need to know more than that, or see a picture?

sections.title: Talking to main Clerk in West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Hello, welcome to the Civic Office of West Ardougne. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is through that door?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: The city warder Bravek is in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go in?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: He has asked not to be disturbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking thanks.

sections.title: Talking to other Clerk in West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Sorry, I don't have time to talk. Bravek needs lots of help running the city.

sections.title: Talking to Edmond after Jethick asks for Elena's picture

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Have you found Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, it's a big city over there. Do you have a picture of Elena?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: There should be a picture of Elena in the house. Please find her quickly, I hope it's not too late.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after Jethick asks for Elena's picture

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Alrena.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Hello, any word on Elena?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Is there anything else I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a picture of Elena?

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Yes. There should be one in the house somewhere. Let me know if you need anything else.

sections.title: Finding Elena
Talking to Jethick after getting Elena's picture
Talking to Jethick again before delivering the book

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hello. We don't get many newcomers around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman from East Ardougne called Elena.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Ah yes. She came over here to help the plague victims. I think she is staying over with the Rehnison family.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: They live in the small timbered building at the far north side of town. I've not seen her around here in a while, mind.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hello, I don't recognise you. We don't get many newcomers around here.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hello. We don't get many newcomers around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a woman from East Ardougne called Elena.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: East Ardougnian women are easier to find in East Ardougne. Not many would come to West Ardougne to find one. Although the name is familiar, what does she look like?

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Ah yes. She came over here to help the plague victims. I think she is staying over with the Rehnison family.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: They live in the small timbered building at the far north side of town. I've not seen her around here in a while, mind.

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: I don't suppose you could run me a little errand while you're over there? I borrowed this book from them, can you return it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll return it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't have time for that.

sections.title: Returning the book to Ted Rehnison

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Go away. We don't want any.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a friend of Jethick's, I have come to return a book he borrowed.

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Oh... Why didn't you say, come in then.

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Thanks, I've been missing that.

sections.title: Talking to Jethick after delivering the book

lines.speaker: Jethick
lines.text: Hello. We don't get many newcomers around here.

sections.title: Talking to Billy Rehnison
Talking to Martha Rehnison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I hear a woman called Elena is staying here.

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: Yes she was staying here, but slightly over a week ago she was getting ready to go back. However she never managed to leave.

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: My daughter Milli was playing near the west wall when she saw some shadowy figures jump out and grab her. Milli is upstairs if you wish to speak to her.

sections.title: Talking to Ted Rehnison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I hear a woman called Elena is staying here.

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Yes she was staying here, but slightly over a week ago she was getting ready to go back. However she never managed to leave.

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: My daughter Milli was playing near the west wall when she saw some shadowy figures jump out and grab her. Milli is upstairs if you wish to speak to her.

sections.title: Talking to Milli Rehnison

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Your parents say you saw what happened to Elena...

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: *sniff* Yes I was near the south east corner when I saw Elena walking by. I was about to run to greet her when some men jumped out. They shoved a sack over her head and dragged her into a building.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which building?

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: It was the boarded up building with no windows in the south east corner of West Ardougne.

sections.title: Talking to Ted Rehnison after questioning Milli

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Any luck finding Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: I wish you luck, she did a lot for us.

sections.title: Talking to Martha Rehnison after questioning Milli

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: Any luck finding Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: I wish you luck, she did a lot for us.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the plague house Elena is in
First attempt after talking to Milli Rehnison

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I'd stand away from there. That black cross means that house has been touched by the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I think a kidnap victim is in here.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Sounds unlikely, even kidnappers wouldn't go in there. Even if someone is in there, they're probably dead by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to check anyway.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You don't have clearance to go in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get clearance?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Well you'd need to apply to the head mourner, or I suppose Bravek the city warder.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I wouldn't get your hopes up though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I fear not a mere plague.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: That's irrelevant. You don't have clearance to go in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get clearance?

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Well you'd need to apply to the head mourner, or I suppose Bravek the city warder.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I wouldn't get your hopes up though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the warning.

sections.title: Attempting before talking to Milli Rehnison, and subsequent attempts

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I'd stand away from there. That black cross means that house has been touched by the plague.

sections.title: Talking to Milli Rehnison after attempting to enter the plague house

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: Have you found Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Milli Rehnison
lines.text: I hope you find her. She was nice.

sections.title: Talking to Head Mourner after attempting to enter the plague house

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: How did you get into West Ardougne? Ah well you'll have to stay, can't risk you spreading the plague outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need clearance to enter a plague house. It's in the south east corner of West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: You must be nuts, absolutely not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a kidnap victim inside!

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: Well they're as good as dead then, no point in trying to save them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a gas mask though...

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: It's not regulation. Anyway you're not properly trained to deal with the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get trained?

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: It requires a strict 18 months of training.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that sort of time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm utterly crazy.

lines.speaker: Head Mourner
lines.text: You're wasting my time, I have a lot of work to do!

sections.title: Attempting to open the door to Bravek before the Clerk lets you in

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Go away, I'm busy! I'm... Umm... In a meeting!

sections.title: Talking to the Clerk Before getting permission to speak to Bravek

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Hello, welcome to the Civic Office of West Ardougne. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need permission to enter a plague house.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Rather you than me! The mourners normally deal with that stuff, you should speak to them. Their headquarters are right near the city gate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try asking them then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely you don't let them run everything for you?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Well, they do know what they're doing here. If they did start doing something badly Bravek, the city warder, would have the power to override them. I can't see that happening though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I speak to Bravek anyway?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: He has asked not to be disturbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is through that door?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: The city warder Bravek is in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go in?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: He has asked not to be disturbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is urgent though! Someone's been kidnapped!

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: I'll see what I can do I suppose.

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Mr Bravek, there's [a man/a lady/someone] here who really needs to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: I suppose they can come in then. If they keep it short.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll leave him alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know when he will be available?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Oh I don't know, an hour or so maybe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the Clerk after getting permission to speak to Bravek

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Bravek will see you now but keep it short!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I won't take much of his time.

sections.title: Talking to Bravek
Talking to Bravek again before getting the hangover cure

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Uurgh! My head still hurts too much to think straight. Oh for one of Trudi's hangover cures!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what's in the cure?

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Hmmm let me think... Ouch! Thinking isn't clever. Ah here, she did scribble it down for me.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: My head hurts! I'll speak to you another day...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is really important though!

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: I can't possibly speak to you with my head spinning like this... I went a bit heavy on the drink again last night. Curse my herbalist, she made the best hang over cures. Darn inconvenient of her catching the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You shouldn't drink so much then!

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Well positions of responsibility are hard, I need something to take my mind off things... Especially with the problems this place has.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think drink is the solution.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: I don't feel well enough to have a philosophical discussion about it right now. My head hurts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what's in the cure?

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Hmmm let me think... Ouch! Thinking isn't clever. Ah here, she did scribble it down for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, goodbye.

sections.title: Mixing the hangover cure
Using chocolate on a bucket of milk
Using snape grass on chocolatey milk
Talking to Bravek after getting the hangover cure

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Uurgh! My head still hurts too much to think straight. Oh for one of Trudi's hangover cures!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Try this.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Ooh that's much better! Thanks, that's the clearest my head has felt in a month. Ah now, what was it you wanted me to do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to rescue a kidnap victim called Elena. She's being held in a plague house, I need permission to enter.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Well the mourners deal with that sort of thing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go speak to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that all anyone says around here?

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Well, they know best about plague issues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you want to take an interest in it at all?

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Nope, I don't wish to take a deep interest in plagues. That stuff is too scary for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see why people say you're a weak leader.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Bah, people always criticise their leaders but delegating is the only way to lead. I delegate all plague issues to the mourners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This whole city is a plague issue!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll talk to the mourners.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They won't listen to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They say I'm not properly equipped to go in the house, though I do have a very effective gas mask.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Hmmm, well I guess they're not taking the issue of a kidnapping seriously enough. They do go a bit far sometimes.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: I've heard of Elena, she has helped us a lot... Okay, I'll give you this warrant to enter the house.

sections.title: Talking to Bravek before getting the warrant

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Thanks again for the hangover cure.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Ah now what was it you wanted me to do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to rescue Elena. She's now a kidnap victim! She's being held in a plague house, I need permission to enter.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Well the mourners deal with that sort of thing...

sections.title: Talking to Bravek after receiving the warrant

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: Thanks again for the hangover cure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a problem, happy to help out.

lines.speaker: Bravek
lines.text: I'm just having a little drop of whisky, then I'll feel really good.

sections.title: Talking to Clerk after meeting Bravek

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: Hello, welcome to the Civic Office of West Ardougne. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is through that door?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: The city warder Bravek is in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go in?

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking thanks.

sections.title: Freeing Elena
Attempting to enter the plague house with the warrant

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: I'd stand away from there. That black cross means that house has been touched by the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a warrant from Bravek to enter here.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: This is highly irregular. Please wait...

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Hey... I've got someone here with a warrant from Bravek, what should we do?

lines.speaker: Mourner (2)
lines.text: Well you can't let them in...

sections.title: Talking to Mourners outside the plague house while holding the warrant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a warrant from Bravek to enter here.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: This is highly irregular. Please wait...

sections.title: Attempting to open the basement door of the plague house without the key

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey get me out of here please!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would do but I don't have a key.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I think there may be one around somewhere. I'm sure I heard them stashing it somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you caught the plague?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: No, I have none of the symptoms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Strange, I was told this house was plague infected.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I suppose that was a cover up by the kidnappers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll look for it.

sections.title: Searching the barrel
Opening the basement door of the plague house while holding the key
Talking to Elena after unlocking the basement door

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, you're free to go! Your kidnappers don't seem to be about right now.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Thank you, being kidnapped was so inconvenient. I was on my way back to East Ardougne with some samples, I want to see if I can diagnose a cure for this plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you can leave via the manhole in the middle of the city.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Go and see my father, I'll make sure he adequately rewards you. Now I'd better leave while I still can.

sections.title: After freeing Elena
Talking to Ted Rehnison after freeing Elena

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: Any luck finding Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, she is safe at home now.

lines.speaker: Ted Rehnison
lines.text: That's good to hear, she helped us a lot.

sections.title: Talking to Martha after freeing Elena

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: Any luck finding Elena yet?

lines.speaker: Martha Rehnison
lines.text: That's good to hear, she helped us a lot.

sections.title: Talking to Alrena after freeing Elena

lines.speaker: Alrena
lines.text: Thank you for rescuing my daughter! Elena has told me of your bravery in entering a house that could have been plague infected. I can't thank you enough!

sections.title: Edmond
Talking to Edmond again before using the Ardougne teleport scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Ah hello. Thank you again for rescuing my daughter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any more of those scrolls?

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Yes, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem.

sections.title: Using the teleport scroll

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Thank you, thank you! Elena beat you back by minutes. Now I said I'd give you a reward. What can I give you as a reward I wonder? Here take this magic scroll, I have little use for it but it may help you.

lines.speaker: Edmond
lines.text: Now I'd recommend you go and see Elena. She'll want to thank you herself. She lives in the house opposite ours.
